clinical_criteria: Smoking status (EVER): Presence of one or more codes from Table 1 at any time in patient's medical record
Smoking status (NEVER): Presence of one or more codes from Table 2 AND no codes from Table 1 ever in patient's medical record
Smoking status (UNKNOWN): Presence of codes from Table 3 AND no codes from Tables 1 or 2, OR no smoking codes available in patient's medical record
algorithm_summary: The main coronary heart disease phenotype algorithm is based on eMERGE Network research where cases are ascertained by either two occurrences of diagnostic codes or one occurrence of a procedural code. Controls are defined as individuals with no diagnostic or procedural codes recorded. The smoking variable component provides a supplementary classification system for smoking history using ICD, CPT, and SNOMED codes to categorize patients as never/ever/unknown smokers based on presence or absence of specific code combinations in their medical records.
